hero.title: Turn any document into learning experiences
hero.subtitle: Like NotebookLM, but open source and LLM-agnostic. Upload PDFs, URLs, or Drive files — generate podcasts, quizzes, summaries, flashcards, presentations, and 20+ formats. Self-host with your own AI keys.

useCases.title: Built for every use case
useCases.subtitle: From classrooms to enterprises to developer workflows.
useCases.educationDesc: Teachers create quizzes, course plans, interactive presentations from their materials. Play live in class or share for self-paced learning.
useCases.enterpriseDesc: L&D teams build onboarding modules, compliance training, knowledge assessments from internal docs. Deploy on your infrastructure.
useCases.developersDesc: Integrate via API, CLI, or MCP server. Generate widgets programmatically. Embed anywhere with JSON Schema.

widgets.title: Widget catalog
widgets.subtitle: 28 formats across interactive, static, media, and pedagogical categories.

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.subtitle: Three steps from document to learning content.
howItWorks.step1Desc: Import documents: PDF, DOCX, web pages, YouTube, PowerPoint.
howItWorks.step2Desc: Chat with AI or use the API to generate quizzes, podcasts, summaries, and more.
howItWorks.step3Desc: Self-paced, live sessions via Qiplim, embed, or export.

studioEngage.title: Studio creates. Qiplim plays.
studioEngage.subtitle: Two apps, one workflow.
studioEngage.studioDesc: Upload documents, chat with AI, generate widgets, edit, compose, share.
studioEngage.engageDesc: Run live sessions with presenter + participants. Real-time interaction, leaderboards, instant feedback.

features.title: Technical features
features.subtitle: Built for developers and IT teams.
features.multiLlmDesc: Mistral, OpenAI, Anthropic, Google. Use your own API keys.
features.ragDesc: Hybrid search with pgvector contextualizes AI generation with your source documents.
features.realtimeDesc: Redis pub/sub, SSE streaming, Ably for Qiplim live sessions.
features.apiDesc: Generative API, CLI, MCP server for AI assistants.
features.jsonSchemaDesc: Every widget is a validated JSON spec. Export, embed, integrate anywhere.
features.selfHostableDesc: Docker, PostgreSQL, full data sovereignty. GDPR compliant.

openSource.title: Open source & self-hostable
openSource.subtitle: Full control over your data, your infrastructure, and your code.
openSource.securityDesc: Audit every line of code. No black box. Community-reviewed, transparent architecture.
openSource.sovereigntyDesc: Your data stays on your servers. No third-party access. Host in your country, your cloud, your rules.
openSource.transparencyDesc: MIT License. See how AI processes your documents. No hidden data collection or tracking.
openSource.controlDesc: Choose your AI providers (BYOK). Configure what's enabled. Update on your schedule. Fork if needed.
openSource.enterpriseDesc: Docker deployment, PostgreSQL, Redis. Production-grade infrastructure you already know.
openSource.communityDesc: Contribute widgets, fix bugs, shape the roadmap. Open governance, open development.

cta.title: Ready to create?
cta.subtitle: Start building interactive components in minutes.

education.subtitle: From high school to university to professional training. Generate quizzes, course plans, and interactive sessions from your teaching materials.

studioEngage.title: Studio creates the content. Qiplim runs the class.
studioEngage.subtitle: Two complementary apps for the full teaching workflow.
studioEngage.studioDesc: Teacher creates content: upload documents, AI generates widgets, edit and compose.
studioEngage.engageDesc: Teacher runs live sessions: presenter controls, students interact in real-time, instant results.

features.title: Built for institutions
features.subtitle: Enterprise-grade features for schools, universities, and training organizations.
features.gdprDesc: Data sovereignty, student data protection. Hosted in France.
features.selfHostDesc: Deploy on your institution's servers. Full control over your data.
features.multiTenantDesc: One instance for your entire region, university, or training network.
features.openSourceDesc: Audit the code, contribute, ensure long-term independence.
features.byokDesc: Use your institution's API keys. Choose your AI provider.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.subtitle: Welcome to the Qiplim Studio documentation. Explore the guides below to get started, understand widgets, integrate the API, or host your own instance.
docs.gettingStartedDesc: Step-by-step guide to create your first studio, import documents, and generate widgets with AI.
docs.widgetTypesDesc: Complete catalog of the 12 available widget types: Quiz, Wordcloud, Roleplay, and more.
docs.apiDesc: API reference for programmatic integration: REST endpoints, OpenAPI spec, MCP server.
docs.selfHostingDesc: Deploy your own Qiplim Studio instance: Docker, PostgreSQL, configuration and troubleshooting.

gettingStartedPage.title: Getting Started
gettingStartedPage.subtitle: Follow this guide to create your first interactive content with Qiplim Studio in minutes.
gettingStartedPage.step1Desc1: Go to the homepage and click Get Started to begin with an anonymous session, or Sign in to create an account with email/password or Google OAuth.
gettingStartedPage.step1Desc2: Anonymous sessions are ideal for quick testing. Create an account to save your studios and access your content from any device.
gettingStartedPage.step2Desc1: From the Dashboard, click New Studio. Give it a name and an optional description.
gettingStartedPage.step2Desc2: A studio is a workspace that contains your source documents and the widgets generated from them.
gettingStartedPage.step3Desc1: In your studio, open the Sources panel and import a document (PDF, DOCX, TXT, etc.). The system will automatically analyze the content and split it into chunks for RAG.
gettingStartedPage.step3Desc2: You can also add web sources (URL) or YouTube. Each source is indexed with embeddings for semantic search.
gettingStartedPage.step4Desc1: Use the integrated Chat to ask questions about your documents. The AI uses RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) to answer based on your sources.
gettingStartedPage.step4Desc2: Configure your AI provider in Settings: Mistral, OpenAI, Anthropic or Google Gemini (BYOK - Bring Your Own Key).
gettingStartedPage.step5Desc1: Ask the AI to generate interactive widgets from your documents: quiz, wordcloud, roleplay, MCQ, ranking, and more.
gettingStartedPage.step5Desc2: Each widget is configurable and editable. You can adjust questions, options, and style directly in the editor.
gettingStartedPage.step6Desc1: Your widgets are ready to use. Export them or integrate them into your presentations via Qiplim Engage for live interactive sessions with your audience.
gettingStartedPage.step6Desc2: Widgets are compatible with the slide and real-time session system of the Qiplim platform.
gettingStartedPage.nextStepSelfHosting: Set up self-hosting for your team
gettingStartedPage.nextStepApi: Check the API reference for integration

widgetTypesPage.title: Widget Types
widgetTypesPage.subtitle: Qiplim Studio offers 12 widget types organized in 3 categories. Each widget can be AI-generated from your documents or created manually.
widgetTypesPage.categoryInteractiveDesc: Widgets that engage participants with direct interactions.
widgetTypesPage.categoryPedagogicalDesc: Structured widgets for learning and content composition.
widgetTypesPage.widgetQuizDesc: Multi-question quiz with automatic correction, scoring and detailed feedback.
widgetTypesPage.widgetQcmDesc: Multiple-choice question with one or more correct answers.
widgetTypesPage.widgetWordcloudDesc: Collaborative word cloud generated in real-time from responses.
widgetTypesPage.widgetPostitDesc: Collaborative notes organized by category, brainstorming style.
widgetTypesPage.widgetRankingDesc: Rank elements by priority or preference.
widgetTypesPage.widgetOpentextDesc: Open-ended question with free response, ideal for reflections.
widgetTypesPage.widgetRoleplayDesc: Roleplay scenario with defined roles and situations.
widgetTypesPage.widgetImageDesc: Image display with caption and zoom, ideal for illustrating content.
widgetTypesPage.widgetPresentationDesc: Set of slides for a structured presentation.
widgetTypesPage.widgetSlideDesc: Individual slide with rich content (text, images, code).
widgetTypesPage.widgetSequenceDesc: Ordered container of child widgets, ideal for step-by-step pathways.
widgetTypesPage.widgetCourseModuleDesc: Composite pedagogical module with objectives, content, and assessment.
widgetTypesPage.kindDescription: Each widget has a kind that determines its structure:
widgetTypesPage.kindLeaf: Standalone atomic widget (Quiz, MCQ, Wordcloud, etc.)
widgetTypesPage.kindComposite: Structured widget with named slots (Course Module)

selfHostingPage.title: Self-Hosting
selfHostingPage.subtitle: Deploy your own Qiplim Studio instance on your infrastructure. The project is open source under MIT license.
selfHostingPage.atLeastOneKey: At least 1 AI API key (Mistral, OpenAI, Anthropic, or Google)
selfHostingPage.step3DockerDesc: The docker-compose includes PostgreSQL (with pgvector) and Redis:
selfHostingPage.step6DevDesc: The studio is accessible at http://localhost:3001.
selfHostingPage.envConfigDesc: Main environment variables. See apps/studio/.env.example for the complete list.
selfHostingPage.dockerComposeDesc: The docker-compose.yml file at the project root defines the services:
selfHostingPage.dockerComposeNote: Note: The pgvector/pgvector:pg16 image includes the pgvector extension for vector embeddings (RAG).
selfHostingPage.cleverCloudDesc: Qiplim uses Clever Cloud (French hosting provider) in production. Required configuration:
selfHostingPage.cleverDb: Database: PostgreSQL add-on with pgvector enabled
selfHostingPage.cleverDeploy: Deployment: Push to the studio branch triggers automatic deployment
selfHostingPage.otherPlatformsDesc: Qiplim Studio is a standard Next.js application. It can be deployed on any platform supporting Node.js: Vercel, Railway, Fly.io, AWS, etc. Make sure PostgreSQL with pgvector and Redis are accessible.
selfHostingPage.troubleDbSolution: Check that Docker is running and port 5433 is not already in use. Use `pnpm docker:status` to check container status.
selfHostingPage.troublePgvectorSolution: Make sure you're using the `pgvector/pgvector:pg16` image (not `postgres:16`). The pgvector extension is required for embeddings.
selfHostingPage.troublePrismaSolution: Re-run `pnpm db:push:studio` to sync the schema. If migration fails, delete and recreate the database: `pnpm docker:down && pnpm docker:up && pnpm db:push:studio`.
selfHostingPage.troubleAiSolution: Check that at least one AI API key is configured in `.env`. Keys are BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) -- the project does not include default keys.

apiPage.title: API Reference
apiPage.subtitle: Integrate Qiplim Studio into your workflows via the REST API. This page centralizes resources for developers.
apiPage.resourceOpenapiDesc: Complete OpenAPI specification of the Qiplim Studio API. Importable into Postman, Insomnia, or any compatible client.
apiPage.resourceMcpDesc: Model Context Protocol server to integrate Qiplim Studio with AI assistants (Claude, GPT, etc.).
apiPage.resourceLlmsTxtDesc: Context file for LLMs. Provides AI models with the information needed to interact with the API.
apiPage.endpointChat: Send a message to AI chat
apiPage.authDescription: The API uses session cookies (BetterAuth). For programmatic integrations, use an API token in the Authorization: Bearer <token> header.

developers.heroSubtitle: Integrate AI-powered educational content generation into your applications. 24 widget types, structured JSON output, multi-provider AI.
developers.quickStartDesc: Generate your first widget in minutes. Obtain an API key from Settings > API Keys, then make a POST request.
developers.widgetTypesDesc: 24 widget types available for generation via the API. Each type accepts a title, optional sources, and type-specific inputs.
developers.apiReferenceDesc: RESTful JSON API. Base URL: https://studio.qiplim.com/api/v1
developers.authenticationDesc: All API endpoints require a Bearer token. API keys start with sk_ and can be created in Settings > API Keys.
developers.rateLimit: Rate limit: 100 requests/hour per API key. Exceeding returns HTTP 429 with a retryAfter field.
developers.generateEndpointDesc: Generate a widget of the given type. Replace {type} with any slug from the widget types list (e.g. quiz-interactive).
developers.fieldSources: Raw text sources as context for generation
developers.fieldProvider: AI provider: mistral, openai, anthropic, google
developers.response400: Invalid request (bad type, missing title, invalid inputs)
developers.typesEndpointDesc: List all available widget types with their input schemas. Useful for dynamic UI generation or MCP tool discovery.
developers.openapiEndpointDesc: OpenAPI 3.1 specification. No authentication required. Dynamically generated from the template registry.
developers.mcpDesc: Qiplim Studio is available as an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server, allowing LLMs like Claude, GPT, or Cursor to generate widgets directly.
developers.stdioTransportDesc: For Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, and other local MCP clients.
developers.httpTransportDesc: Streamable HTTP for remote or web-based MCP clients.
developers.toolListTypes: List available widget types and their schemas
developers.toolGenerateWidget: Generate an educational widget from source text
developers.toolSearchSources: Search indexed sources within a studio
developers.llmsTxtDesc: Qiplim Studio provides a /llms.txt file following the llms.txt standard. This Markdown file gives LLMs all the context they need to interact with the API: endpoints, widget types, authentication, and MCP configuration.
developers.footerLicense: Open source under AGPL-3.0 -- Made by Pando Studio
